string.text: The .45 calibre full metal jacket round has enjoyed popularity for more than a hundred years thanks to its heavy bullet and a relatively small propelling charge. As a result, the bullet in this round is relatively slow but highly accurate and provides considerable stopping power.
Special 12.7x55mm PS12B cartridge with subsonic armor-piercing bullet weighing 18 grams with heat-strengthened steel core, with double-layer half-shell, in brass casing.
Special 12.7x55mm PS12 cartridge with subsonic 33 grams weighted bullet with lead core and bimetallic jacket, in brass casing.

This unusual .45 ACP calibre round is comprised of a jacketed soft point bullet with an expanding hollow and a steel core. It is a combination of jacketed expanding soft point and armour piercing bullets, benefiting, from the advantages of both and, suffering from their downsides.

.44 Remington Magnum or just .44 Magnum is a rimmed, large-bore cartridge originally designed for revolvers and quickly adopted for carbines and rifles. Thanks to its high power it can be used for hunting.
.44 Panthera Magnum Boom is a special and very rare modification of .44 magnum ammo with very high power. Due to it's high power it is not made for usage by civillians .
.50 Action Express (also as .50 AE, 12.7 x 33, 12,7x32,6 мм) is an ammo made in 1988 specifically for Desert Eagle pistol, one of the most powerful pistol in the world.
Standard 12-calibre round filled with 6 mm shot. Highly lethal at close range. Only suitable for use with smoothbore weapons.
This heavy 12 calibre slug round is normally used for hunting big game, but is also good for other purposes. Instead of piercing, this hefty slug simply crushes through armour.
Military 5.45x39 7H22 calibre armour-piercing round for automatic rifles of Warsaw Pact countries. The bullet contains a hard-alloy armour piercing penetrator. Effective against body armour.

Military 5.45x39 7H10 calibre improved piercing round for automatic rifles of Warsaw Pact countries. Provides better accuracy than the 7.62 calibre round thanks to reduced recoil and a 100 meter increase in range.
A common NATO military 5.56x45 calibre armour piercing round, known under its SS190/M855 index, widely used by Western security agencies and special services. Effective against body armour.

A standard issue NATO 5.56x45 calibre round specially designed for accurate automatic fire. Provides good ballistic characteristics, having been designed on the basis of a hunting round. Widely known under its SS109 index.
This ammo is made on the base of the .338 Win Mag shell which length was slightly increased and head got tighten so it can fit new bullet.
Soviet 7.62x39 calibre round designed for automatic rifles. Ordinary bullet with steel core.
Soviet 7.62x39 calibre round designed for automatic rifles. Bullet with heat-strengthened core, has better armor piercing characteristics.
A common NATO rifle round. Calibre - 7.62mm. Ordinary bullet with lead core (NATO SS77).
A common NATO rifle round. Calibre - 7.62mm. Bullet with steel core, has better armor-piercing (NATO M61).
A 7.62 calibre sniper round with a steel penetrator specially designed for the SVD. Unlike the machine gun round, the 7H1 provides tight groupings and excellent penetration characteristics.
A 7.62 calibre sniper round with a steel penetrator specially designed for the SVD. Unlike the machine gun round, the 7H1 provides tight groupings and excellent penetration characteristics
Cheap 7.62 calibre machine gun round. Its design makes it unsuitable for sniper rifles, so it is used only with the PRM machine gun.

Soviet rimless bottleneck pistol cartridge that proven itself in times of WW2 and was still popular in 1990's mafia wars in Russia. It got widely spread in zone because of it cheap price and availability. It hss solid armor piercing, but in comparison with modern calibres it has less stopping power.
A full metal jacket round with a steel penetrator and a lead core under a metal jacket. Provides good stopping power but is ineffective against body armour.

This round contains a bullet with enhanced stopping power, while its European +P+ designation indicates "overpressure ammunition" with a 30% increase in muzzle velocity. Ineffective against body armour.

This basic 9x19 calibre round with a jacketed bullet has good characteristics while being simple to produce. The round is popular due to the fact that international conventions prohibit the use of expanding ammunition in combat. Ineffective against body armour.

This 9x19 calibre jacketed hollow point bullet with an expanding hollow provides high stopping power and low ricochet propensity. Used by police and for self-defence. Ineffective against body armour due to its low penetration potential.

The SP-6 is a subsonic assault rifle round with a jacketed soft point bullet with a hard steel core that protrudes from the casing. Requires a silencer for stealth firing. Used by special services of Warsaw Pact countries. Effective against body armour.

The SP-5 is a subsonic sniper round with a bimetallic casing. The round contains a steel core with a gap behind it that is filled with lead. Requires a silencer for stealth firing. Used by special services of Warsaw Pact countries.
Special sniper ammo made for shooting at long ranges, by today it is also widely used among hunters and sports shooters.
A 40mm round for underbarrel grenade launchers. It is compatible with most weapon models in service with NATO. The effective fragmentation casualty radius is approximately 5 meters.
Upgraded type of ammo for a 40mm grenade launchers that is used by NATO. It is made for hitting targets that are under cover.
Fragmentation warhead for anti-personnel warfare used with the RPG 7 grenade launcher. The main purpose of the warhead is to kill enemies in cover outside the range of hand grenades using the RPG 7.

Regular fragmentation grenade for the GP-25 Koster under-barrel grenade launcher. This round has a 5 meter kill zone. The VOG-25 grenade is also used in the Groza and RG-6 revolver-type grenade launcher.
PSO-1M2 is a 4x24 telescopic sight manufactured in Russia by the Novosibirsk instrument-making factory (NPZ Optics State Plant)
Optics that meant to be used at the night time. Can be equipped on Dovetail mantle.
A scope with variable zoom. Can be equipped on picatinny rail.
A scope with variable zoom. Can be equipped on picatinny rail.
Optics that meant to be used at the night time. Can be equipped on picatinny mantle.
Optics that meant to be used at the night time. Can be equipped on picatinny mantle.
Can be equipped on picatinny mantle.
Scope meant for usage in middle-ranged combat. can ve equipped on Dovetail mantle

F-1 is an anti-personnel fragmentation defensive grenade. It is based on the French F1 grenade and contains a 60 g (2.1 oz) explosive charge (TNT). The total weight of the grenade with the fuze is about 600 g (21 oz).

Single-shot, muzzle-loaded underbarrel grenade launcher for automatic rifles of the Warsaw Pact member countries. The trigger mechanism is double-action, with a manual safety and an automatic firing block when improperly installed.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Grenades: VOG-25, VOG-25P.
The M203 is a single-shot 40 mm under-barrel grenade launcher designed to attach to a rifle.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Grenades: M433, M397A1.
Grenade launcher made specifically for L85A2 AR. \n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Grenades: M433, M397A1.
Grenade launcher made specifically for G36 AR. \n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Grenades: M433, M397A1.
Grenade launcher made specifically for FN F2000 AR. \n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Grenades: M433, M397A1.
An offensive hand grenade with a small kill zone of 5 meters. Often used by stalkers to "smoke out" mutants due to its relatively low cost.
A hand smoke grenade. Used to put up smoke screens. Once the grenade is thrown it takes 10 seconds for the smoke screen to expand.

The ASh-12 assault rifle (“Avtomat Shturmovoy, sample 2012” or ShAK-12) is a Russian assault rifle that was developed in 2012. Its creation was due to the need to modernize the standard weaponry for the Russian armed forces.

A sawed-off hunting shotgun with two side-by-side barrels, making it lighter and more compact than a full shotgun. One of the most popular weapons among bandits due to its combination of ease of concealment and extreme effectiveness in close combat %c[0,153,255,255](change fire mode for duo-shot)%c[default].
TOZ-66 double barreled shotgun. Has much better accuracy than the sawed-off variant %c[0,153,255,255](change fire mode for duo-shot)%c[default].
A hand-made pistol based on the Desert Eagle design. The components are finely tuned to achieve incredible accuracy for a handgun of this type.

Raging Bull is a revolver manufactured by the Brazilian Taurus International firearm company. In its larger calibers it is marketed as a hunter's sidearm because it is a potent weapon with plenty of stopping power.

The RSh-12 or 12.7 mm calibre assault revolver. It is considered and recognised as the largest calibre personal close combat weapon. It was developed and designed in 2000 in Tula, in the Central Committee of the Institute of Sporting and Hunting Weapons.

This assault rifle combined with a grenade launcher, which is similar to the AK series, was specially developed for rapid response units. The main differences are its 9x39 calibre ammunition, bullpup layout and extensive configuration options. This weapon is highly valued by military stalkers due to its ability to kill enemies wearing body armour behind improvised cover at a distance during urban combat, as well as its excellent stopping power.

A modification of the standard OTS-14-1A (7.62x39 mm caliber), created specifically for special forces operating in the Zone. It features picatinny rails, tritium iron sights, as well as an anatomical pistol grip and a special vertical grip. The main drawback is that a silencer cannot be installed.

A modified model of a pistol from the first half of the 20th century, which is as reliable, powerful and accurate as the original. Pistols of this series deserve their reputation as one of the best handguns of the 20th century and are in great demand in the Zone.

This submachine gun has gained popularity around the world thanks to its reliability, ease of use and accuracy. Over the last few decades the MP5 was in service with special armed forces and police units worldwide. Not surprisingly, having flooded the global black markets, it eventually made its way to the Zone.

Heckler Koch UMP is a submachine gun developed and manufactured by Heckler & Koch. Heckler & Koch developed the UMP as a lighter and cheaper successor to the MP5, though both remain in production. Designed as a next generation close quarter combat weapon, it benefits from a higher calibre, rate of fire and accuracy.

MP9 is a submachine gun chambered for the 9x19mm Parabellum cartridge that is designed and manufactured by Brugger & Thomet. The MP9 is a development of the Steyr TMP. The design of the TMP was purchased from Steyr in 2001. Differences from the TMP include a stock that folds to the right side of the weapon, an integrated Picatinny rail, and a new trigger safety.
A modified variation of PKM that was made for legendary machinegunner called Vova Vist, who heroically defended the route between cordon and swamps but went missing for unknown reasons.
Silenced version of APS made for Spetsnaz. Although the only difference from APS is the barrel that can be equipped with a silencer

AA-12 (Auto Assault-12) is an automatic shotgun created by Maxwell Atchisson based on his earlier design, the Atchisson Assault Shotgun. Its most notable features are low recoil and a fully automatic-only firing mode. It is fed from a 20 drum magazine.

This revolver-type grenade launcher uses six rifled front-loading barrels, identical to under-barrel launcher attachments. The drum is also front-loading. Widely used for rapid suppression of enemy firing positions and, when in the open, attacking defended fixed targets or fighting particularly tough monsters.

This belt-fed machine gun is a portable modification with a complex two-stage feeding system. Despite its weight and low precision, this LMG has found admirers in the Zone. Used primarily to fight off outnumbering enemy forces during faction conflicts and clear out mutant lairs.
Tactical version of well-known pistol among stalkers. It has a rail adapter for laser sights and flashlight, and is equipped with 10 round magazine. Rare thing in the zone.

SIG SG 552 is a compact AR specially made for a police forces. The return mechanism has been moved to the rear of the receiver housing and its recoil spring is guided in a way analogous to that of the AK. Can be equipped with piccatiny rails.

HK416 A5 is a next step in development of HK416 chambered in 5.56x45 mm NATO. The main difference from previous generations are better gas tube, receiver, improved mag tube and other little tweaks made for better reliabillity and ergonomics. This variant is equipped with 60 round drum from Magpul company.

Sniper rifle, produced since 1999. Most parts of the sniper rifle are interchangeable with the standard SG 550 rifle. However, there are some significant differences. SG 550 Sniper rifle is equipped with heavy 650mm barrel. The buttstock is equipped with adjustable cheek piece, as well as adjustable rubber buttstock. The pistol grip is similar to HK PSG-1 sniper rifle, and is height and angle adjustable. The fire mode is single shot only.

The Benelli M4 is a semi-automatic shotgun produced by Italian firearm manufacturer Benelli Armi SpA. There is an piccatiny rail adapter mantled on receiver. The shotgun equipped with pistol grip and telescopic stock.

An army sniper rifle that has been on the market for more than fifty years and has earned its reputation for exceptional reliability and ease of use. The SVD is normally used by the army to improve the effective range of its units, but stalkers respect this weapon for its armour-piercing properties, which allow it to penetrate mutant hide and army body armour.
The same SVD sniper rifle, but with foldable stock. Equipped with 20 round mag, anatomical handgrip and piccatiny rails.
Molot OP-SKS is a version of the SKS chambered in 7.62x39 made especially for hunters. The main difference is that the OP-SKS has built-in Dovetail mantle.
This shortened sniper rifle with an increase rate of fire is the next step in the evolution of the SVU sniper rifle. This weapon used to belong to Max, the legendary guardian of Freedom.

This extremely common over-and-under hunting shotgun can offer better protection against mutants than a pistol, thanks to its accuracy and stopping power. Used mostly by rookies on the outskirts of the Zone %c[0,153,255,255](change fire mode for duo-shot)%c[default].
An unknown weapon, the firing mechanism of which has not been established. The weapon appears faulty - squeezing the trigger doesn't do anything.

Impeccable reliability and durability combined with excellent accuracy are the main noteworthy features of this weapon. Its minor drawbacks include its considerable size, relatively high center of gravity and massive bolt, which make it difficult to conceal but do not outweigh the weapon's positive features. The weapon is especially popular with the Zone's veterans.

The FN FNX pistol is a series of semi-automatic, polymer-framed pistols manufactured in Columbia, South Carolina, by FN America (branded FNH USA), a division of Fabrique Nationale d'Herstal. The pistol is chambered for the 9x19mm Parabellum, .40 S&W, and .45 ACP cartridges. It was introduced in 2009 as the successor to the FN FNP. This variant already has a built-in silencer.

A special military sniper rifle purpose-built for silent, flashless sniping during special operations where early detection of the shooter may be critical. The Vintorez provides high armour-piercing capability and stopping power, as well as being equipped with an integrated sniper and optical scope. Highly rated by many stalkers despite its small magazine.
Vintorez-M is an upgraded version of VSS. The main difference is that this variant has better characteristics. "M" in the name stands for "Modernized".

SR-3 Vikhr is a Russian 9x39mm compact assault rifle. Heavily based on the AS Val, but lacks an integral suppressor, has a newly designed folding stock and charging handle for ease of concealed carry.

A Western smoothbore shotgun that is extremely popular around the world thanks to its amazing reliability and faster reloading speed. Particularly valued for its functionality by the Zone's hunters. All of its parts are coated with an anti-corrosion compound.

The Remington Model 870 is a pump-action shotgun manufactured by Remington Arms Company, LLC. It is widely used by the public for shooting sports, hunting and self-defense, as well as by law enforcement and military organizations worldwide.

A modern assault rifle developed as a replacement for the ageing AK-74. The main difference is its high speed 2-round burst firing mode, which allows two bullets to be fired at exactly the same spot on a target up to 100 meters away. This significantly increases the likelihood of hitting the target in comparison to the 1 round firing mode, which considerably improves kill potential, stopping power and armour piercing capability..

AKS-74N (AKS-74 Night Rifle Kalashnikova FoldingStock 74 - "AKS-74 Nochnoy Avtomat Kalashnikova Skladnoy 74") is an assault rifle of 5.45x39 mm caliber, equipped with a metal shoulder rest with side folding, designed primarily for use by airborne infantry. The key design difference from the standard AKS-74 is the side "dovetail" mount for optical and night sights.

АК-12 or 6P70 is a prototype presented in 2012. The prototype model of the AK-12 is very different from its predecessors ergonomically. It features a telescoping buttstock that is in-line with the barrel for better recoil control and a stock latch, allowing for it to be folded to either side of the rifle. It has a rubber height-adjustable cheek piece and butt plate. The cocking handle is moved forward and can be attached to both sides for ambidextrous use. The receiver is hinged and more rigid with a Picatinny rail for mounting optics.

AK-103 is an AK-100 derivative of the AK-74M that is chambered for the 7.62x39mm M43 cartridge, similar to the AKM. The AK-103 can be fitted with a variety of sights, including night vision and telescopic sights, plus a knife-bayonet or a grenade launcher like the GP-34.

Despite being similar to submachine guns in size, weight and tactical purpose, this weapon is classified as an assault rifle due to the fact that its internal mechanism is identical to that of the AKS-74. The weapon is notable for its mobility in close quarters and high armour-penetration capacity, while its drawbacks are its short effective range, despite the relatively long bullet range, a tendency to overheat and high ricochet propensity.

The AK-105 is a short barrel, carbine version of the AK-74M rifle, originally developed to replace the shorter barrelled AKS-74U. The AK-105 is chambered in 5.45x39mm ammunition and is used domestically by the Russian Army in contrast to other AK-100 series rifles.

An improved version of a classic automatic pistol, which passed all the major armed conflicts of the 20th century with flying colours and entered the new millennium with confidence. Produced by dozens if not hundreds of arms manufacturers around the world in both standard and modified configurations. The weapon's only drawback, its mediocre magazine capacity, is largely compensated by the hard-hitting ammo it uses.

The ultimate hand cannon - bulky, heavy and highly lethal. Developed as a specialized weapon for hunting medium-sized and large game, as well as for shooting competitions involving firing at steel body targets at distances over 50 meters. Its limited use in the Zone can be attributed to its price, size and weight, which are all double those of other pistols.

The Steyr AUG (German: Armee-Universal-Gewehr, lit. 'army universal rifle') is an Austrian bullpup assault rifle chambered for the 5.56x45mm NATO intermediate cartridge, designed in the 1960s by Steyr-Daimler-Puch, and now manufactured by Steyr Arms GmbH & Co KG.
The Steyr AUG A3 features a Picatinny rail on top of the receiver and an external bolt release. This variant has equipped with 42 round magazine.

The FN F2000 is a 5.56x45mm NATO bullpup rifle, designed by FN Herstal in Belgium. Its compact bullpup design includes a telescopic sight, a non-adjustable fixed notch and front blade secondary sight. The weapon has fully ambidextrous controls, allowed by a unique ejection system, ejecting spent cartridge casings forward and to the right side of the weapon, through a tube running above the barrel.
FN F2000 Tactical is identical to FN F2000, but lacks built-in optics which place was taken by piccatiny rails. Equipped with 40 round mag.

A Ukrainian-made pistol predominantly used by law enforcement organisations. Provides higher accuracy, better ergonomics and has a larger magazine than the PM. These pistols make it into the Zone in large numbers thanks to the sale of defective weapons, which suffer from technical miscalculations in early design models. Despite this, the pistol is in demand among stalkers thanks to its good combat characteristics.

OTS-33 Pernach is a Russian 9x18 Makarov machine pistol, derived from the 5.45 mm OTs-23 Drotik machine pistol. The Pernach is an automatic pistol designed to replace the Stechkin APS in various special OMON units within the Russian police, the Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs (MVD) and other paramilitary units.

A new addition to the Western-made assault rifle family, which differs significantly from previous models in the series built on an automatic foundation with a semi-open bolt assembly, this weapon is more like American automatic gas-operated assault rifles with a short-stroke gas piston system. These changes ensure that the rifle is a first-rate example of a modern weapon - lightweight, reliable and ergonomic.
HK MG36 is an automatic rifle variant of the G36 equipped with a heavier barrel for increased thermal performance and cook-off resistance.

A British assault rifle designed with a bullpup configuration. Its main advantages are high single-shot accuracy and great potential for upgrading. When firing in bursts, accuracy drops sharply, and the automatic mechanism tends to jam.
M4 is a 5.56x45mm NATO, select-fire, gas-operated, magazine-fed assault rifle and carbine developed in the United States during the 1980s. It is a shortened version of the M16A2 assault rifle.

This pistol was developed in the second half of the 20th century on the basis of the PM for use by special forces and scouting units. The front of the pistol's barrel is covered with an integrated silencer, while the bolt mechanism is short, which prevents a back-moving spring from being located inside it, and it is therefore situated in the weapon's handle. Morally obsolete, the weapon is still effective in the right hands.

This Soviet legacy is the most common pistol in the Zone and is one of the best compact self-defence handguns available. It is small, highly reliable, easy to use and maintain in working order. It also suffers from several drawbacks, including its short range, low firing accuracy and poor stopping power, while also losing out to modern pistols in its class in terms of weight and magazine size.

A smoothbore combat revolver-type shotgun intended to provide extreme firepower for militarized units. The weapon benefits from a large magazine and a high effective rate of fire, but its bulkiness and considerable weight, combined with a long reload time, are the price to be paid for these advantages. Rare in the Zone and is used primarily to clear closed spaces of mutants.

his grenade launcher from the 1960s made its way into the Zone from army depots. The weapon is a smoothbore single-shot recoilless launch system with an open-rear tube. Although its huge size and weight make it an unlikely choice for stalkers, given its huge destructive power and ease of use, it has found admirers in the Zone.

The only remaining mass produced model of a globally renowned pistol, this weapon has earned a reputation for its excellent ergonomics and accuracy, and while its magazine is relatively small, it benefits from powerful ammo and a mechanism that functions like a Swiss watch. Some believe the SIP-t M200 to be the best mass produced .45ACP calibre handgun there is and it has certainly got a following among experienced stalkers and mercenaries.

This special purpose smoothbore automatic shotgun was designed in the second half of the 20th century and comes with pump-action and self-cocking firing modes. Used as an all-purpose weapon by the police and assault troops. Notable for its reliability and tactical flexibility. Despite its large weight, complex mechanism and considerable cost it is in demand in the Zone due to its effectiveness against mutants.

A modernized version of the SVD sniper rifle in a compact bullpup design made on the order of rapid response forces. It allows faster precision fire and is considerably lighter, while its single shot firing accuracy at short and medium range is similar to that of its predecessor. These qualities have earned it the admiration of countless stalkers.

A modified version of the silent Vintorez sniper rifle, this assault rifle was designed as a special forces weapon. The rifle's primary purpose is effective fire against enemies employing modern personal protection equipment without disclosing the shooter's location. Thanks to its integrated silencer and 9x39 calibre round with a heavy subsonic bullet, the weapon's noise and muzzle flash have been reduced to a minimum.

A super-accurate hi-tech weapon known as item 62, this rifle was developed in secret laboratories located inside the Zone. The weapon operates by accelerating bullets to extremely high speeds using a system of electromagnets, which provides incredible stopping power with virtually no recoil. The weapon is powered by using encapsulated artefact fragments in the batteries.

Beretta pistols enjoy the reputation of a relatively reliable, albeit somewhat cumbersome weapon, around the world. In service with armed forces and police across the ocean, as well as various military and law enforcement organisations worldwide. The pistol is equally popular with members of the underworld, thanks to its powerful round and magazine capacity. Berettas found in the Zone are usually dated, probably from retired stock.

An excellent Western pistol known for its reliability, high capacity magazine and good accuracy, as well as being among the most ergonomic handguns in its class. One of the most widely used pistols in the Zone, both among professionals and rookies who can afford it.
Batteries that use a encapsulated fragment of the Flash artefact as their power source. Manufactured using hi-tech equipment.
These lower-capacity cells use encapsulated fragments of the Flash artefact as their power source. Their lower capacity is due to makeshift production conditions and lack of specialized equipment.
PFM-1 is a scatterable high explosive anti-personnel land mine of Soviet and Russian production. It is also known as a Green Parrot or Butterfly Mine
Grenade for GP-25 grenade launcher. Before exploding it "jumps" at the height of 1meter, which makes it more deadly.
EOTech is a world leader in producing holographic sights for weapons.
Aimpoint Micro T-1 is a tactical red dot sight for weapon, known for it's small size.
RDS made for a quick aiming.
PSО-1М2-01 is a scope made for VSS "Vintorez" and AS VAL rifles. Optics are meant to be shot from 100 to 400 meters.
Silencer for pistols chambered in .45 ACP.
Silencer made for an AK's chambered in 5.45. Thanks to the construction, there's no need to use subsonic rounds.
Silencer made for an AK's chambered in 7.62. Construction meant for shooting subsonic rounds only.
Silencer made for a SVD sniper rifle.
Silencer made for weapon chambered in 5.56x45 NATO.
Silencer made for weapons chambered in 7.62x51 NATO. Can be equipped only on "5T1" adapter.
Silencer made for SMG's chambered in 9x19 mm.
Silencer produced by Hexagon company, meant for usage on 12 gauge shotguns.
Silencer for a MP9 SMG, Produced by "Brugger & Thomet".
Series of army optics for middle-ranged combat. Usually can be equipped on piccatiny rail.
Russian optics made for middle-ranged combat, meant for AR's and LMG's. Has a zoom of 2.8x
Russian Holographic sight. If necessary, iron sights can be used along side with it.
EKP-8-02 Kobra RDS meant for hitting fast moving targets
PU is a soviet scope made in 1940.
Optics with various zoom that supposed to replace ACOG scope.
DVL-10 М1 is a light, precise and silent sniper rifle made. Made by Lobaev Arms.

SV-98 - is a Russian bolt-action sniper rifle designed by Vladimir Stronskiy. According to the manufacturer the SV-98 sniper rifle is designed to engage various targets at a range up to 1,000 m (1,100 yd).

SV-98М - In 2013 an upgraded SV-98 variant was developed by Izhmash featuring an adjustable aluminum skeleton type stock. Izhmash expects the upgraded variant to be more economical to produce and offer improved maintainability and accuracy of fire.

The Five-seven pistol was developed in conjunction with the FN P90 personal defense weapon and the FN 5.7x28mm cartridge. Its low weight, high magazine capacity, powerful rounds and high reliabillity makes the Five-seveN one of the best pistols.

Mosin-Nagant, known officially as the 3-line rifle M1891 and informally in Russia and the former Soviet Union as Mosin's rifle. Developed from 1882 to 1891, it was used by the armed forces of the Russian Empire, the Soviet Union and various other states. It is one of the most mass-produced military bolt-action rifles in history, with over 37 million units produced since 1891. In spite of its age, it has been used in various conflicts around the world up to the present day.

5.7x28 is a small-caliber, high-velocity, smokeless-powder, rebated, non-tapered, bottleneck, centerfire cartridge designed for pistols and personal defense weapons (PDW) uses, manufactured by FN Herstal.
Silencer made specifically for FN Five-Seven.
Silencer made specifically for FN P90.
PP-19 Bizon is a 9x18mm Makarov submachine gun developed in 1993 by the Russian company Izhmash. It features a distinctive helical magazine under the barrel that can hold 64 rounds.

The FN P90 is a submachine gun chambered for the 5.7x28mm cartridge, also classified as a personal defense weapon, designed and manufactured by FN Herstal in Belgium. The 5.7x28 caliber was created especially for the P90.

American company DSA (David Selvaggio Arms) manufactures a copy of the FAL called the FAL DSA SA58 FAL that is made with the same Steyr-Daimler-Puch production line equipment as the StG-58. Civilian clients are limited only to semi-automatic configuration, but military and law enforcement clients can procure select-fire configuration that is capable of firing in full auto with cyclic rate of fire of around 650-750 rounds per minute. The SA58 FAL can use any metric-measurement FAL magazines, which come in 5, 10-, 20-, or 30-round capacities.

Marksman version of SA-58. In comparison to original it has 10 round magazine, improved flash hider, sniper stock, anatomical handgrip, new receiver, sniper optics and increased damage in exchange for lower fire rate.

Saiga-12К is a short version of the Saiga-12 shotgun, patterned after the Kalashnikov series of rifles and named after the Saiga antelope native to Russia. Like the Kalashnikov rifle variants, it is a rotating bolt, long-stroke gas piston operated firearm that feeds from a square magazine.

Modernized version of the Saiga-12 that was produced for some unknown group of military stalkers. It has: 10 round mag, anatomical handgrip, picatinny rail, improved bolt, GK-02 compensator and an automatic firemode.
MP-133 is a russian pump-action shotgun, which is used for hunting, self-defense and by guards.
МР-153 is a 12 gauge gas-operated semi-automatic shotgun manufactured by Kalashnikov Concern. Quite useful for hunting mutants.

Accuracy International AWM-F (Arctic Warfare Magnum-Folding) is a bolt-action sniper rifle manufactured by Accuracy International designed for magnum rifle cartridges. Is designed as a dedicated long range sniper rifle.
Hunting carbine made on the base of AKM. Chambered in 7.62x51. Primarily meant for hunters.
Remington 700 is a series of bolt-action centerfire rifles manufactured by Remington Arms since 1962. It is a development of the Remington 721 and 722 series of rifles, which were introduced in 1948.

FN SCAR-H is an Assault Rifle made by FN Herstal for SOCOM. This model is chambered by 7.62x51 mm NATO rounds. The FN SCAR is effective at any distance, although the small magazine size (20 rounds) can be an issue when fighting a large group of enemies.

The RPK, sometimes inaccurately termed the RPK-47, is a Soviet 7.62x39mm light machine gun that was developed by Mikhail Kalashnikov in the early 1960s, in parallel with the AKM assault rifle. It was created to standardize the small arms inventory of the Soviet Army, where it replaced the 7.62x39mm RPD machine gun.

The RPK-74 was introduced in 1974 together with the AK-74 assault rifle and chambered for the new 5.45x39mm intermediate cartridge. It was derived from the AK-74 rifle, with modifications that mirror those made to the AKM to create the RPK.
Slightly upgraded version of RPK-74 that is equipped with 60 round mag and plastic furniture.
The RPK-16 light machine gun, chambered in 5.45x39, is a modern replacement for RPK-74. This model is equipped with 96 drum magazine.
Silencer for Glock series pistol. Can only be equipped on a special adapter.

The Glock 17 is the original 9x19mm Parabellum model, with a standard magazine capacity of 17 rounds, introduced in 1982. Known as the "AK of the pistol world", this pistol has proven itself to be very reliable.
Glock 18C is a modification of the Glock 17 with auto-fire mode. This model is equipped with 33 stick mag.

The SR-1MP variant is a further modernization kit of the SR-1M which adds two additional groves onto the dust cover allowing for the user to mount a Picatinny rail adapter onto the lower dust cover section of the firearm for adding accessories such as flashlights or laser sight system. This kit also comes with a threaded barrel to allow the option of attaching a quick detach suppressor. Chambered in quite rare in the zone 9x21 ammo rounds.

SR-2 Veresk is a Russian submachine gun designed to fire the 9x21mm Gyurza pistol cartridge. The SR-2M is a modified version that also has a forward pistol grip under the handguard, with a protrusion to protect the shooter's hand from muzzle blast and accidents. Its stock can be fully folded even with a "red dot" sight installed.
Silencer for pistols chambered in 9x21mm rounds
Silencer for a SR-2MP SMG. Very rare in the zone.
Hand-made silencer for Mosin-Nagant, made by unknown stalker.

The 9x21mm Gyurza (Гюрза, Russian for "blunt-nosed viper") round is a Russian handgun round designed to defeat body armor that was developed by TsNIITochMash for its SR-1 Vektor semi-automatic pistol and SR-2 Veresk submachine gun.
Silencer for APB pistol. Incompatibale with other weapons.
Ordinary silencer for a SV-98 sniper rifle.
Unique variant of TOZ-34 with a PU scope. Can't be loaded with buckshot because of custom barrels %c[0,153,255,255](change fire mode for duo-shot)%c[default].

Colt M45A1 is a magazine-fed, recoil-operated, single-action, semiautomatic pistol chambered for the .45 ACP cartridge. It is a variant of the M1911 for use by the Force Recon Element of the United States Marine Corps' Marine Expeditionary Units from 1985 until 2022.

TT-33 is a Soviet semi-automatic pistol. It was developed during the late 1920s by Fedor Tokarev as a service pistol for the Soviet Armed Forces and based on the earlier pistol designs of John Moses Browning, albeit with a number of detail modifications to simplify production and maintenance. Very popular among bandits.
Compensator for VPO-101 hunting rifle. Positions itself as the compensator, although it works as a suppressor too.
Soviet 4x Zoom scope. Meant for usage on Groza.
Grenade launcher made specifically for Groza. Uses VOG-25 grenades.
RGM-40 grenade launcher is a stand-alone version of the Russian GP-30 grenade launcher with a telescoping stock, AK-type pistol-grip and flip-up tangent sights.

HK M320 is the U.S. military's designation for a new single-shot 40 mm grenade launcher system to replace the M203 for the U.S. Army, while other services initially kept using the older M203. The M320 uses the same High-Low Propulsion System as the M203.

The M249 SAW is manufactured in the United States by the subsidiary FN Manufacturing LLC, a company in Columbia, South Carolina (FN America), and is widely used in the U.S. Armed Forces. The weapon was introduced in 1984 to address the lack of sustained automatic fire capability at the squad level. The M249 SAW combines the rate of fire of a machine gun with the accuracy and portability of an assault rifle.

PP-19-01 Vityaz is a 9x19mm Parabellum submachine gun developed in 2004 by Russian small arms manufacturer Izhmash. It is based on the AK-74 and offers a high degree of parts commonality with the AK-74. The gun is directly developed from the PP-19 Bizon.

The Walther WA 2000 is a semi-automatic bullpup sniper rifle produced by the Carl Walther GmbH Sportwaffen company from 1978 to 1988. The WA2000 was introduced to the markets in 1981 and was produced in three different calibers. Production of the rifle was limited and it was shortly stopped because it was too expensive to achieve widespread sales and not robust enough for military use as a sniper rifle.
The Desert Tech MDR is a gas operated, conventional rotary bolt bullpup rifle. The gas system features a gas piston located above the barrel and a 6 position manual gas regulator.

The Desert Tech MDR is a gas operated, conventional rotary bolt bullpup rifle. The gas system features a gas piston located above the barrel and a 6 position manual gas regulator. This model is equipped with 50 drum mag

The semi-automatic G28 is chambered for 7.62x51mm NATO and has a factory warrantied accuracy of 45 mm dispersion at 100 meters (0.45 mil or 1.5 moa) when fired with 10 rounds using OTM/HPBT/Sierra Match King ammunition. The G28 features STANAG 4694 NATO Accessory Rails that are backwards-compatible with the STANAG 2324/MIL-STD-1913 Picatinny rails.
QD 7.62x51 is a supressor for G28 made by Brugget & Thomet.

A knife from Bars A-2607.
 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit strength: %c[0,204,0,0]low 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit length: %c[0,204,0,0]low

ОTS-04 battle knife. Was developed in 1980-1990 for usage in police forces.
 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit strength: %c[0,255,170,0]medium 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit length: %c[0,255,170,0]medium

M9 (M9 Bayonet)is an American multi-purpose knife and bayonet officially adopted in 1986 by the United States Armed Forces for the M16 rifle.
 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit strength: %c[0,255,170,0]medium 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit length: %c[0,204,0,0]low

MTech USA Xtreme MX-8054 is a tactical battle knife.
 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit strength: %c[0,0,153,25]high 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit length: %c[0,255,170,0]medium

KM2000 is a german battle knife. 
 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit strength: %c[0,0,153,25]high 
%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Hit length: %c[0,0,153,25]high
Browning B725 is the latest version of legendray b25 double barreled shotgun. This gun is extremely handy to use and very reliable.
Galil ACE is a series of assault rifles and battle rifles originally developed and manufactured by Israel Weapon Industries (IWI).
Galil ACE is a series of assault rifles and battle rifles originally developed and manufactured by Israel Weapon Industries (IWI).

TDI KRISS Vector is a series of weapons based upon the parent submachine gun design developed by the American company KRISS USA, formerly Transformational Defense Industries (TDI). They use an unconventional delayed blowback system combined with in-line design to reduce perceived recoil and muzzle climb, invented by French engineer Renaud Kerbrat.
Classic double-barreled sawed-off. Loads with 12 gauge.
Hunters dream. Double-barreled classic as it is. Loads with 12 gauge.
USAS-12 is an automatic shotgun manufactured in South Korea by Daewoo Precision Industries since the 1980s.

A super-accurate hi-tech weapon known as item 62, this rifle was developed in secret laboratories located inside the Zone. The weapon operates by accelerating bullets to extremely high speeds using a system of electromagnets, which provides incredible stopping power with virtually no recoil. The weapon is powered by using encapsulated artefact fragments in the batteries. %c[0,153,255,255](Press firemode change button to open-close condition monitor)

MTs-255 is a Russian revolver shotgun fed by a 5-round swing-out cylinder. It is produced by the TsKIB SOO, Central Design and Research Bureau of Sporting and Hunting Arms. The MTs255 is unique in that the forearm extends nearly all the way back to the cylinder. The shotgun is reloaded in a manner consistent with that of most modern revolvers, by unlocking the cylinder, and swinging it away from the frame to the left and down.

Anti-personnel fragmentation percussion-remote hand-held offensive grenade. It is designed to engage manpower in offensive combat. The grenade is equipped with a contact fuze that detonates on impact. It is additionally protected against explosion when the grenade falls dangerously close to the person who threw it.

Hand-held anti-personnel defensive percussion grenade. It is designed to engage manpower in defensive combat. The grenade is equipped with a contact fuse, which is triggered on impact. The grenade is additionally protected against explosion if the grenade falls dangerously close to the person who threw it.
